claimText: The Great Nicobar Island Project (GNIP) was conceived by NITI Aayog, with the Andaman and Nicobar Islands Integrated Development Corporation (ANIIDCO) serving as its nodal agency.
evidenceExcerpt: GNIP was conceived by NITI Aayog; ANIIDCO is the nodal agency.
examUse: Statement-based question on the institutional framework and implementation of major infrastructure projects.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) as the nodal agency
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways as the conceptualizing body

claimText: The administration in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands has cited the Protection of Aboriginal Tribes Act, 1956 (PAT) to bypass the Forest Rights Act, 2006 (FRA) mandate requiring Gram Sabha consent for forest land diversion.
evidenceExcerpt: PAT 1956 allows unilateral forest land diversion, conflicting with FRA 2006's mandate for Gram Sabha consent.
examUse: Statement-based question on the conflict between different tribal and environmental protection laws regarding land diversion.
distractorHooks: Gram Sabha consent is mandatory and cannot be bypassed under any law in A&N Islands
The Forest Conservation Act of 1980 was used to bypass the FRA

claimText: Galathea Bay, the proposed site for the GNIP transshipment port, is a Ramsar Site and a key nesting site for the endangered Leatherback Sea Turtle.
evidenceExcerpt: Galathea Bay is a Ramsar Site and a key nesting site for the endangered Leatherback Sea Turtle.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on protected areas, Ramsar sites, and flagship species habitats.
distractorHooks: Olive Ridley turtles as the primary nesting species at Galathea Bay
Galathea Bay is located in the Lakshadweep Islands
Galathea Bay is a UNESCO World Heritage site but not a Ramsar site

claimText: Tribal Councils in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands have limited advisory and executive powers, unlike the autonomous councils established under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution.
evidenceExcerpt: Tribal Councils in A&N Islands have limited advisory/executive powers, unlike Sixth Schedule autonomous councils.
examUse: Comparison question evaluating the powers of Tribal Councils in Union Territories versus Sixth Schedule areas.
distractorHooks: Tribal Councils in A&N Islands enjoy the same autonomy as Sixth Schedule councils
Tribal Councils in A&N Islands have absolute veto power over infrastructure projects
